entities.raw_quote: ...to send me a certain spirit who is expert in teaching of all the sciences...

entities.name: hoopoe (bird)

entities.raw_quote: ...the blood of a bird, perhaps a hoopoe, and evidently the magician is to use the heart of this bird as a writing instrument...

entities.name: sword

entities.name: circle (ritual)

attributes.description: drawn on cloth; double concentric band within square; inscribed with demon names and directions

entities.raw_quote: The circle required for this first experiment is a single band inscribed within a square. Inscribed in and around the band are the names of seven spirits and the four cardinal directions...

entities.name: tablet (for writing)

attributes.description: used to record spirit’s name and order

entities.raw_quote: ...you must have a tablet, and must write down all that he tells you.

entities.raw_quote: In the church of Saint Simeon in the diocese of Trier there was a little schoolboy...

entities.name: ars notoria

attributes.description: medieval magical tradition for acquiring knowledge via angelic invocation

entities.raw_quote: ...the later medieval magic of the so-called ars notoria, which uses prayers and invocation of angels to gain mastery of the liberal and mechanical arts.

relationships.type: held by demon, returned at end of instruction

summary: This chunk details a ritual from the Munich Handbook (CLM 849) for acquiring knowledge through demonic conjuration, focusing on the invocation of three demon kings—Apolin, Maraloth, and Berith—to send a teacher-spirit. It outlines the use of bird blood and heart to inscribe names on cloth, the construction of a ritual circle, sleep-based spirit communication, and symbolic exchange of a sword representing authority over knowledge. Kieckhefer compares this practice with Jewish angelic magic, the ars notoria, and Caesarius of Heisterbach’s story of a schoolboy who made homage to the devil.
key_passages: ...the markings on this cloth are to be made with the blood of a bird, perhaps a hoopoe, and evidently the magician is to use the heart of this bird as a writing instrument...
The circle required for this first experiment is a single band inscribed within a square. Inscribed in and around the band are the names of seven spirits and the four cardinal directions.
...you must have a tablet, and must write down all that he tells you.
O Apolin, Maraloch, Berith, Sathan, Beliath, Belzebuc, [and] Lucifer, 1 implore you to command the master - here naming his name - to come to me tomorrow before sunrise...
